item.title: Test of fast logarithm function
item.author: kimjg@astro.princeton.edu (Jeong-Gyu Kim)
item.description: Python implementation of logapprox fucntion from ctypes import ( Union, Array, c_float, c_int32, cdll, CDLL ) class u_type(Union): _fields_ = ("f", c_float), ("i", c_int32) # load printf function from Dynamic Linked Libary libc.so.6 if os.uname().sysname == 'Darwin': libc = CDLL(cdll.LoadLibrary('libc.dylib')._name) else: libc = CDLL(cdll.LoadLibrary('libc.so.6')._name) printf = libc.printf if __name__ == "__main__": # initialize union _32bitsdata = u_type() # set values to chunk _32bitsdata.f = 10.0 # and print it #printf(b"Data in 32 bits: %.

item.title: Simulation Movies
item.author: kimjg@astro.princeton.edu (Jeong-Gyu Kim)
item.description: Some are research-grade products and some are made just for fun. All simulations are run with the Athena code unless otherwise noted. Destruction of Giant Molecular Clouds by UV Radiation Feedback (Kim et al. 2020) Destruction of Giant Molecular Clouds by UV Radiation Feedback (Kim et al. 2018) Thermal Instability

item.title: Cloud Dispersal by Radiation Feedback
item.author: kimjg@astro.princeton.edu (Jeong-Gyu Kim)
item.description: Almost all stars in the present-day Universe are believed to form in clusters embedded in giant molecular clouds (GMCs). It has long been known to observers that star formation in GMCs is a slow and inefficient process, in the sense that star formation rate is much more smaller than what would be expected from the free-fall (pure gravitational) collapse andthat clouds turn only a small fraction of gas mass into stars during their lifetime.

item.title: About Me
item.author: kimjg@astro.princeton.edu (Jeong-Gyu Kim)
item.description: I am a Lyman Spitzer, Jr. Postdoctroal Fellow postdoc at the Department of Astrophysical Sciences at Princeton University. Combining theory and numerical simulations, my researh focuses on understanding the dynamical evolution of molecular clouds under the influence of stellar feedback. During my PhD, I implemented a numerical algorithm for adaptive ray tracing that efficiently solves the radiative transfer problem involving multiple point sources. This has been extensively used to study destruction of molecular clouds by UV radiation feedback and escape of radiation in diverse star-forming environments.
